title: Study of RMC-5127 in Patients With Advanced KRAS G12V-Mutant Solid Tumors

power_analysis.assessment: N/A — Phase 1 trial, not powered for efficacy

adaptive_designs.description: Adaptive design detected: Types identified: Dose-Finding / Phase I.

safety_adverse_events.ae_ascertainment: Not explicitly described (assumed standard investigator reporting)
safety_adverse_events.safety_monitoring: Investigator-reported with sponsor oversight (no DSMB mentioned)

eligibility.text: At least 18 years old and has provided informed consent.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures compliance with legal requirements for clinical trials involving human subjects.

eligibility.text: Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) performance status of 0 or 1.
eligibility.justification: This criterion helps ensure the patient's ability to tolerate treatment and participate in the trial.

eligibility.text: Pathologically documented, locally advanced or metastatic KRAS G12V-mutated solid tumor malignancy.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures that the study population is homogeneous and allows for a meaningful comparison of treatment effects.

eligibility.text: Received and progressed or been intolerant to prior standard therapy (including targeted therapy) appropriate for tumor type and stage.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures that the patient has a history of treatment failure, which may impact their ability to tolerate study drug.
This criterion helps ensure that the study population is homogeneous and allows for a meaningful comparison of treatment effects.

eligibility.text: Adequate organ function (bone marrow, liver, kidney, coagulation).
eligibility.justification: This criterion helps ensure that the patient's body can tolerate the study drug and its potential side effects.
This criterion ensures that the patient is able to swallow the study drug, which may be a requirement for some drugs.
This criterion helps ensure that the patient's brain is healthy enough to tolerate study drug and its potential side effects.

eligibility.text: Prior therapy with KRAS G12V inhibitor or direct RAS-targeted therapy (eg. degraders and/or inhibitors).
eligibility.justification: This criterion helps ensure that the patient has a history of treatment failure, which may impact their ability to tolerate study drug.

eligibility.text: Any conditions that may affect the ability to take or absorb study drug.
eligibility.justification: This criterion helps ensure that the patient's body can tolerate the study drug and its potential side effects.

eligibility.text: Major surgery within 28 days prior to receiving study drug(s).
eligibility.justification: This criterion helps ensure that the patient's body is fully recovered from previous surgery, which may impact their ability to tolerate study drug.

eligibility.text: Patient is unable or unwilling to comply with protocol-required study visits or procedures.
eligibility.justification: This criterion helps ensure that the patient can follow the study's protocols and adhere to its requirements.
